R3 Enterprise Corp.

Integrated services

Specialty scopes delivered under one standard of execution.

R3’s service platform is built to reduce coordination risk and give customers one accountable partner across insulation, firestopping, heat tracing, removable insulated covers, and support services that make sense for the work.

Industrial / Mechanical Insulation

Insulation for piping, ducts, vessels, tanks, and equipment delivered with planning, labor quality, and documentation discipline built into the job.

  • ✓Maintenance, retrofit, and project work
  • ✓Documented installation methods
  • ✓Condensation and thermal-performance focus

Firestopping

Passive fire protection delivered with labeling, photos, closeout support, and inspection-ready documentation.

  • ✓Listed systems and traceability
  • ✓Photos and inspection support
  • ✓Clean coordination with other trades

Heat Tracing

Freeze protection and temperature-maintenance scopes integrated with insulation for reliability, efficiency, and safer operations.

  • ✓Integrated electrical coordination
  • ✓Testing and verification
  • ✓Built for long-term performance

Removable Insulated Covers

Custom covers for valves, flanges, pumps, and equipment that improve safety, maintenance access, and energy control.

  • ✓Low-disruption entry scope
  • ✓Repeatable facility mapping
  • ✓Strong maintenance value

Project environments

Work built for active facilities, complex systems, and demanding schedules.

R3 supports owners, facility teams, and contractors who need specialty work executed safely, documented clearly, and coordinated professionally.

Industries served

Focused on industrial, commercial, regulated, and mission-critical work.

R3 works in environments where documentation, safety culture, and execution quality are expected from the start, including industrial facilities, healthcare, data centers, infrastructure, and commercial buildings.

Industrial Manufacturing

Plants and process environments where uptime, safety, and execution discipline directly affect operations.

Healthcare Facilities

Live-facility work where documentation, coordination, cleanliness, and respect for operating constraints matter.

Data Centers

Mission-critical projects where condensation control, turnover quality, and mechanical reliability protect performance.

Power & Infrastructure

Projects and maintenance scopes where safety systems, schedule certainty, and dependable execution are expected from day one.

Commercial Projects

High-rise, mixed-use, and other commercial building work where coordination, schedule awareness, and clean execution are essential.

R3 Enterprise Corp.

Regional footprint

Rooted in Western Kentucky. Active across Tennessee and positioned to support critical work where needed.

R3’s operating footprint is grounded in Western Kentucky with practical reach across all of Tennessee and the surrounding region. The company also supports trusted partners in other parts of the country when critical work needs to be completed safely, professionally, and on time.

Western Kentucky roots

The company story is anchored in Western Kentucky with an operating model designed around where crews live, where relationships are strongest, and where regional demand continues to grow.

Tennessee-wide focus

R3 is positioned to serve work across Tennessee while keeping mobilization practical for field supervision, crew stability, and schedule support.

Critical partner support

R3 follows strong relationships and trusted partners where it makes sense, including travel to other regions of the country when critical projects need experienced support to stay on track.

Work ethic

Reliable, Respect, Results.

That line reflects how R3 approaches the work: reliable execution, respectful conduct in the field, and results customers can trust.

Transparency, safety, and professional conduct are part of the finished product. The goal is work that holds up operationally and relationships that hold up over time.

We plan the work before the crew mobilizes.

We communicate early when scope, schedule, or access changes.

We treat documentation as part of the finished product.

We respect the customer, the jobsite, and the people working beside us.

We do not chase growth that compromises safety, quality, or execution.

We aim to earn repeat work by being dependable under pressure.

Core values

Values that show up in the field, not just on a wall.

R3’s values shape how the company manages people, communicates with customers, and delivers work in complex environments.

Safety

Incident-free is the standard. Safe work is productive work, and leadership owns the outcome.

Relationships

Trust, integrity, respect, and open communication shape how R3 works with customers, labor partners, and suppliers.

Entrepreneurial Spirit

Initiative, accountability, follow-through, and practical problem solving are part of the culture.

Sense of Belonging

People are expected to perform at a high level and be treated with dignity while they do it.

Profitability

Margin discipline supports reinvestment, workforce stability, safety, and disciplined growth.

Achievement & Legacy

The goal is work customers trust, a reputation people respect, and results that stand up over time.

Execution approach

Planning, communication, and accountability carried through the work.

R3 is organized around discipline, planning, communication, and accountability so customers get a more consistent and professional project experience.

Work is planned deliberately before crews mobilize.

Safety and quality remain the standard when schedules tighten.

Field leadership is expected to communicate early and clearly.

Documentation is treated as part of the scope of work.

Scope changes and cost risks are surfaced early and handled transparently.

Time-and-material work is handled with the same discipline as project work.

R3 Enterprise Corp.

Leadership

Experience that supports the brand promise.

Public-facing leadership focused on operations, governance, planning, safety, and disciplined project execution.

Greg Rushing

President

Greg brings more than 25 years of hands-on experience in industrial and commercial mechanical insulation, with leadership across business development, estimating, contract negotiation, project execution, strategic growth, and client relationship management.

  • ✓Led regional and large-project operations across Kentucky, Tennessee, and national work, with direct oversight of estimating, workforce management, scheduling, and safety performance.
  • ✓Deep operating background in mechanical insulation systems, removable insulation covers, scaffolding coordination, industrial services, and energy-focused applications such as 3E Plus modeling.
  • ✓Prior experience includes project work across automotive, battery, healthcare, hospitality, manufacturing, data center, chemical, and power environments, along with zero-incident safety leadership and asbestos / lead compliance experience.
Cell: 270-871-7543
Office: 270-900-8780
Email: gregr@r3-enterprise.com

David D. Jones, Esq.

Vice President

David brings more than 40 years of executive, legal, compliance, financial, and governance experience, helping R3 align contract strategy, risk management, policy development, and regulatory discipline with the realities of field execution.

  • ✓Provides executive legal and compliance leadership for contracts, labor and employment matters, governance, enterprise risk, workplace investigations, safety-sensitive issues, and day-to-day legal exposure across the business.
  • ✓Background includes founding and leading legal and compliance businesses, advising operating companies and investment advisers, and handling corporate law, mergers and acquisitions, government contracting, and commercial disputes.
  • ✓Supports policy development, operational controls, payroll and billing compliance, records practices, confidentiality expectations, and other systems that help protect the company and its customers.
Cell: 210-540-1681
Office: 270-900-8780
Email: davidj@r3-enterprise.com

Jason Kelley

Operations Manager

Jason adds deep operations, project management, safety, and field leadership experience across industrial contracting, supporting work from acquisition and preconstruction through execution, closeout, and long-term customer support.

  • ✓Experience includes director-level operational oversight, project acquisition, estimates, proposals, scheduling, staffing, contract administration, change management, and direct support of site teams as senior management representative.
  • ✓Previously led branch and project operations with responsibility for contract review, invoicing, collections, forecasting, budget tracking, and active project coordination.
  • ✓Brings a strong EH&S foundation, site safety leadership, industrial project coordination, and hands-on experience supporting maintenance, abatement, scaffold-related work, and complex field execution.
Cell: 615-910-8002
Office: 270-900-8780
Email: JasonK@r3-enterprise.com

FAQ

Answers that help move the conversation forward.

Clear answers around fit, scope, geography, and approach so customers can move into the first conversation with confidence.

R3 is positioned around dependable execution, transparent communication, safety leadership, and documentation discipline. That means clearer scope conversations up front, stronger coordination in the field, and a finished product that supports long-term performance rather than short-term fixes.
Yes. R3’s platform is built to coordinate insulation, firestopping, heat tracing, removable insulated covers, and support services that make sense for the work. That integrated approach helps reduce coordination risk, supports schedule performance, and gives customers one team accountable for execution quality across related scopes.
Yes. The company is focused on environments where safety, professionalism, documentation, and schedule discipline are expected from the start. That includes industrial plants, healthcare facilities, data centers, infrastructure work, and commercial projects where clean execution and dependable follow-through matter.
R3 is rooted in Western Kentucky and active across all of Tennessee and the surrounding region. The company also supports trusted partners in other parts of the country when critical work needs to be completed safely, professionally, and on time. Travel decisions are made carefully, with an emphasis on the right relationships, the right manpower, and the ability to execute the work to R3 standards.
